questions.q: Which numbered President of India was R. Venkataraman?
questions.explanation: Ramaswamy Venkataraman served as the eighth President of India.

questions.q: In which year did R. Venkataraman begin his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: He assumed office as President in 1987.

questions.q: In which year did R. Venkataraman complete his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: His presidential term concluded in 1992.

questions.q: What does the 'R.' in R. Venkataraman stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Ramaswamy Venkataraman.

questions.q: The presidency of R. Venkataraman is described as falling within an era of what kind of politics?
questions.explanation: His tenure coincided with an era of coalition politics.

questions.q: During his tenure, R. Venkataraman is noted for appointing several holders of which office?
questions.explanation: During his tenure he appointed several Prime Ministers amid coalition politics.

questions.q: The President's office held by Venkataraman is the constitutional head of what?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the constitutional head of state.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did R. Venkataraman serve as President?
questions.explanation: He served from 1987 to 1992, a term of about five years.

questions.q: Which decade did most of R. Venkataraman's presidential term fall within?
questions.explanation: His term began in 1987 and ran into 1992, mostly in the late 1980s.

questions.q: Which office did R. Venkataraman hold immediately before becoming President?
questions.explanation: He served as Vice-President of India before being elected President.

questions.q: As Vice-President before his presidency, Venkataraman served as ex-officio Chairman of which House?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The President, the office Venkataraman held, is elected by an electoral college of which members?
options: Elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: The President is elected by an electoral college of elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies.

questions.q: As President, R. Venkataraman was the supreme commander of which body?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the supreme commander of the armed forces.

questions.q: Venkataraman's presidency immediately followed that of which President?
questions.explanation: He succeeded Giani Zail Singh, the seventh President.

questions.q: Why was the appointment of Prime Ministers especially significant during Venkataraman's term?
options: No single party held a clear Lok Sabha majority
questions.explanation: In the coalition era no single party held a clear majority, making the President's role in inviting Prime Ministers significant.

questions.q: The normal term of office of the President, which Venkataraman held, is how many years?
questions.explanation: The President is elected for a term of five years.

questions.q: The President whom Venkataraman became is a constituent part of which legislative body?
questions.explanation: Parliament consists of the President and the two Houses.

questions.q: Which sequence correctly orders Venkataraman's two highest constitutional offices?
questions.explanation: He was Vice-President before becoming President.

questions.q: The Vice-Presidency held by Venkataraman is described as which constitutional office in rank?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President holds the second-highest constitutional office in India.

questions.q: Venkataraman's presidency was immediately followed by that of which President?
questions.explanation: Shankar Dayal Sharma, the ninth President, succeeded Venkataraman.

questions.q: The coalition era during Venkataraman's term is generally associated with which feature of national politics?
options: Governments formed by alliances of parties
A two-term limit on Prime Ministers
The abolition of the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: The coalition era saw governments formed by alliances of several parties.

questions.q: As Vice-President, Venkataraman would act as President in which situation?
options: During a vacancy in the office of President
When the Lok Sabha is dissolved
questions.explanation: The Vice-President acts as President during a vacancy in that office.

questions.q: The President's role of appointing the Prime Minister, exercised by Venkataraman, flows from which Article on the appointment of ministers?
questions.explanation: Article 75 provides that the Prime Minister is appointed by the President.

questions.q: The Vice-President's role as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, which Venkataraman held earlier, is set out in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 64 makes the Vice-President the ex-officio Chairman of the Council of States.

questions.q: The President's power to promulgate ordinances, available to Venkataraman in office, is contained in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.q: The executive power of the Union, exercisable through the President whom Venkataraman became, is vested by which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President.

questions.q: The office of Vice-President, held by Venkataraman, is provided for under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 63 provides that there shall be a Vice-President of India.

questions.q: The Vice-President, the office Venkataraman held, is elected by an electoral college consisting of whom?
options: Members of both Houses of Parliament
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is elected by the members of both Houses of Parliament.

questions.q: Which statement about the chronology of Venkataraman's offices is accurate?
options: He served as Vice-President immediately before becoming President
He was President before serving as Vice-President
He never held the office of Vice-President
questions.explanation: He served as Vice-President directly before being elected President.

questions.q: When a government loses majority support during a coalition era like Venkataraman's term, the President's discretion chiefly concerns which act?
options: Inviting a person who can command a majority to form the government
questions.explanation: In such situations the President's role centres on inviting someone who can command a Lok Sabha majority to form the government.
